excel_structure.notes: Excel file contains 1 test case with detailed multi-step test scenario. The Steps column contains multiple numbered steps with embedded expected results. Test case is marked as Complex with Visual (UX) + functional categorization.

test_case_metadata.title: Billing Cycle Period card
test_case_metadata.description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card functionality on Print and Payment History page for paused Instant Ink subscription plans with various page limits (25/50/100/300/700 pages). Validates display of pause information, complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page count accuracy.
test_case_metadata.sub_module: Dashboard - Extended > Pause Plan > Print and Payment History page with 25/50/100/300/700 pages plan
test_case_metadata.author: Nitish

custom_fields.section: Print and Payment History page with 25/50/100/300/700 pages plan
custom_fields.section_hierarchy: Instant Supply > Regression Test > 2 - Medium > Dashboard - Extended > Pause Plan > Print and Payment History page with 25/50/100/300/700 pages plan
custom_fields.analysis_status: To be discussed: Specific Ink printer? 4.Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan. Manual execution for pages on overview to be performed and verified the same

preconditions.raw_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
preconditions.interpreted_meaning: A printer must be enrolled in the HP Instant Ink service with a subscription plan of at least 50 pages per month. This requires either API-based enrollment setup or manual enrollment before test execution. The printer must be successfully registered and associated with a valid Instant Ink account.

implied_components.reason: Need API helper to enroll printer with specific plan programmatically for test setup
Need printer ID, account credentials, and plan details (50+ pages) for enrollment
Need to verify enrollment was successful before proceeding with test
Need utility to check current enrollment status of printer

preconditions.raw_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
preconditions.interpreted_meaning: The Instant Ink subscription associated with the enrolled printer must not have any free trial months or promotional free months active. This ensures the billing cycle behaves as expected for a regular paid subscription. May require API call to verify subscription details or database query.

implied_components.reason: Need API helper to retrieve subscription information and verify no free months
Need to assert that free months field is null or zero
Need utility to validate subscription is in correct state for testing

preconditions.raw_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
preconditions.interpreted_meaning: The Instant Ink subscription status must be in 'subscribed' state (active and not cancelled, paused, or expired). This is a critical precondition that determines whether the subscription is actively billing. Requires verification through API or database query before test execution.

implied_components.reason: Need API helper to retrieve current subscription status
Need to assert subscription status equals 'subscribed'
Need expected status value 'subscribed' for comparison

preconditions.raw_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
preconditions.interpreted_meaning: Before the main test steps begin, the user must navigate to the appropriate location (CIT modal or Instant Ink page) and click either the 'Pause Plan' button or 'Pause Instant Ink' link to initiate the plan pause action. This changes the subscription state from 'subscribed' to 'paused'. This is both a navigation and state-change precondition.

implied_components.reason: Need page object for the Instant Ink page where pause link exists
Need page object for CIT modal if pause button is there
Need locator for Pause Plan button on CIT modal
Need locator for Pause Instant Ink link
Need method to click pause plan button or link
Need method to navigate to Instant Ink page
Need to verify plan pause action completed successfully
Need to wait for pause confirmation message or status change

Refresh the page and verify that now TWO progress bars are displayed: one for Complimentary pages (fully filled) and a new one for Additional pages (showing 5 pages used).

flow_sequence.action_summary: Navigate to Print and Payment History page
Verify Billing Cycle Period card shows no pause info initially
Shift time by 32 days and trigger billing charge
Return to page and verify pause info now displayed
Verify Complimentary pages progress bar and tooltip
Print 6 pages and verify progress bar updates
Print additional pages to exceed limit and verify Additional pages section appears
Final verification of complete state with full complimentary pages and total count

flow_analysis.critical_path: Navigate to Print and Payment History → Verify initial state (no pause info) → Time shift 32 days → Verify pause info displayed → Verify Complimentary pages section → Print pages within limit → Print pages exceeding limit → Verify Additional pages section → Verify final complete state

automation_notes.complexity_justification: This is a very complex test case involving multiple factors: (1) Requires backend API integration for time manipulation and print simulation which are not standard UI automation tasks, (2) Tests responsive behavior with different interactions for desktop vs mobile/tablet, (3) Involves dynamic UI elements that appear conditionally (Additional pages section only appears after exceeding limit), (4) Requires visual validation of progress bar colors and fill percentages which needs CSS property checks, (5) Tests state changes across multiple page refreshes with data persistence, (6) Involves tooltip interactions with hover and click based on device type, (7) Requires precise text matching with special formatting like '0 of 10(Pause Plan) used', (8) Has complex preconditions requiring specific subscription state setup, (9) Tests billing cycle behavior which is time-dependent, (10) Requires verification of multiple progress bars with different colors and behaviors simultaneously.

risks.risk: Backend API availability and authentication - Test heavily depends on API calls for time manipulation and print simulation. If API is not available or authentication fails, test cannot proceed.
risks.mitigation: Implement robust API authentication mechanism with token refresh. Add API health checks before test execution. Have fallback mechanism or skip test if API unavailable.

risks.risk: Dynamic element locators - Progress bars, tooltips, and conditional elements like Additional pages section may have dynamic IDs or classes making locators fragile.
risks.mitigation: Use data-testid attributes if available. Prefer CSS selectors based on stable attributes. Implement retry logic for element location.

risks.risk: Timing issues - Multiple page refreshes and API calls create timing dependencies. Progress bars may not update immediately after API calls.
risks.mitigation: Use explicit waits with proper conditions. Wait for specific element states (visible, text contains) rather than fixed delays. Implement polling mechanism to verify data updates.

risks.risk: Responsive testing complexity - Different interactions for desktop vs mobile/tablet (hover vs click) increases test complexity and potential for device detection failures.
risks.mitigation: Implement reliable device type detection. Test on actual mobile devices or emulators. Have separate test configurations for different device types.

risks.risk: Visual validation accuracy - Verifying progress bar colors (black vs yellow) and fill percentages requires CSS property checks which may vary across browsers.
risks.mitigation: Use normalized color values (hex or RGB). Allow small tolerance in percentage calculations. Test across target browsers to establish baseline.

risks.risk: Test data dependencies - Test requires specific printer enrollment, subscription state, and plan configuration. If preconditions are not met, test will fail.
risks.mitigation: Implement robust test data setup using API helpers. Verify all preconditions before starting main test steps. Use dedicated test accounts/printers.

risks.risk: State persistence across refreshes - Test assumes data persists correctly across page refreshes. Cache issues or backend delays could cause stale data.
risks.mitigation: Implement cache clearing before refreshes. Add verification that data has actually updated by checking timestamps or version numbers if available.

risks.risk: Tooltip interaction flakiness - Tooltips may not appear consistently on hover, especially in headless browser mode.
risks.mitigation: Use JavaScript executor to trigger tooltip display if hover fails. Implement retry logic for tooltip interactions. Consider using click instead of hover even on desktop for reliability.

risks.risk: Text format variations - Expected text like '0 of 10(Pause Plan) used' may have spacing or formatting variations across environments.
risks.mitigation: Use flexible text matching (contains, regex) instead of exact equals where appropriate. Normalize whitespace before comparison.

risks.risk: Billing cycle simulation accuracy - Time shift of 32 days may not perfectly simulate real billing cycle behavior if there are edge cases or business logic complexities.
risks.mitigation: Validate time shift behavior with backend team. Test with different time shift values. Verify billing charge actually occurs by checking backend state.

recommendations: Implement comprehensive Page Object Model with separate classes for InstantInkPage, PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage, and CITModal. Each page object should encapsulate all locators and action methods for that page.

Create dedicated API helper class for all backend operations (enrollment, time shift, billing trigger, print simulation). This should handle authentication, request/response parsing, and error handling.
Implement device detection utility that can reliably determine device type and conditionally execute hover vs click actions. Consider parameterizing tests to run on multiple device types.
Create custom assertion methods for progress bar validation that can check both color and fill percentage in a single call. This should handle color normalization and percentage tolerance.

Use test data management framework (e.g., JSON files, database, or API) to provide all test data including printer IDs, subscription IDs, expected tooltip messages, and page count values. Do not hardcode test data in test scripts.

Implement comprehensive wait strategy using explicit waits with custom expected conditions. Create reusable wait utilities for common patterns like 'wait for text to change', 'wait for progress bar to update'.
Add extensive logging at each step to track test execution flow, API responses, element states, and assertion results. This is critical for debugging failures in this complex test.
Implement screenshot capture not just on failure but also at key checkpoints (after each major state change) to create visual test evidence.
Create cleanup utilities that can reset test state (unpause subscription, reset page counts) to ensure test can be run repeatedly without manual intervention.
Consider breaking this large test into smaller focused tests if possible, or at least implement clear test phases with independent verification points.
Implement retry logic for flaky operations like tooltip interactions and page refreshes with data verification.
Use configuration management to externalize all environment-specific values (URLs, timeouts, credentials) and device-specific settings.
Add API response validation to ensure backend operations actually succeeded before proceeding with UI verification.
Implement visual regression testing for progress bars if possible, using screenshot comparison tools to validate 'displayed well' requirements.
Create test data setup and teardown hooks that run before and after test to ensure clean state. Use API helpers for efficient setup/teardown.
special_handling_notes: This test requires BACKEND API ACCESS which is not typical for UI automation. Ensure API endpoints are documented and accessible from test environment.
The test involves TIME MANIPULATION which affects billing cycles. Coordinate with backend team to ensure test environment supports time shifting without affecting other tests or production data.
RESPONSIVE TESTING is required - test must run on both desktop and mobile/tablet with different interaction patterns. Consider using separate test configurations or parameterized tests.
VISUAL VALIDATION of progress bar colors and fill percentages requires CSS property checks. Standard text-based assertions are insufficient.
The test has CONDITIONAL UI ELEMENTS - Additional pages section only appears after exceeding complimentary limit. Locators for these elements must handle element not found gracefully initially.
TOOLTIP INTERACTIONS may not work in headless browser mode. Consider running this test in headed mode or using JavaScript to trigger tooltips.
The test involves MULTIPLE PAGE REFRESHES with data persistence verification. Implement robust data update verification rather than assuming refresh shows latest data.
EXACT TEXT MATCHING is required for some assertions (e.g., '0 of 10(Pause Plan) used'). Be careful with whitespace, special characters, and formatting.
The test has COMPLEX PRECONDITIONS that must be set up via API before test execution. Consider implementing a dedicated setup phase or fixture.
BILLING SIMULATION is a critical part of this test. Ensure test environment has proper billing simulation capabilities and does not affect real billing systems.
The test validates 'displayed well' which is subjective. Work with product team to define specific acceptance criteria for visual validation.
Consider implementing VISUAL REGRESSION TESTING using screenshot comparison tools for the 'displayed well' validations.
The test involves PROGRESS BAR PERCENTAGE CALCULATIONS. Implement calculation utilities and allow small tolerance for rounding differences.
DEVICE TYPE DETECTION is required for conditional actions. Ensure detection mechanism is reliable across test environments.
The test has LONG EXECUTION TIME due to multiple API calls and page refreshes. Optimize where possible but maintain test integrity.
automation_notes.framework_compatibility_notes: This test case analysis is structured for the Aura automation framework. All component names follow Aura naming conventions (snake_case for elements/methods, PascalCase for page objects). The analysis assumes Aura framework provides: (1) Page Object Model support with base page class, (2) API testing capabilities with request/response handling, (3) Explicit wait utilities with custom expected conditions, (4) Configuration management for environment-specific values, (5) Test data management capabilities, (6) Screenshot capture on failure, (7) Logging framework, (8) Device/browser management for responsive testing. If Aura framework lacks any of these capabilities, additional infrastructure will need to be built. The component_needs_summary section provides detailed mapping requirements that can be used to search Aura codebase for existing implementations or identify gaps that need new development.
